instruction: Block the following domains by adding entries to /etc/hosts that redirect them to 127.0.0.1: ads.example.com, tracker.example.net, malware.badsite.org. Before making changes, create a backup at /etc/hosts.bak. Do not remove any existing entries in /etc/hosts. After modification, create ~/Desktop/hosts_block_report.txt with exactly: blocked=3 and backup=/etc/hosts.bak on separate lines.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu manpage - hosts(5) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man5/hosts.5.html)

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'cp /etc/hosts /etc/hosts.osworld_original; sed -i "/ads.example.com\|tracker.example.net\|malware.badsite.org/d" /etc/hosts; rm -f /etc/hosts.bak'; rm -f ~/Desktop/hosts_block_report.txt

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command: blocked_ads='false'; grep -q '127.0.0.1.*ads.example.com' /etc/hosts && blocked_ads='true'; blocked_tracker='false'; grep -q '127.0.0.1.*tracker.example.net' /etc/hosts && blocked_tracker='true'; blocked_malware='false'; grep -q '127.0.0.1.*malware.badsite.org' /etc/hosts && blocked_malware='true'; preserved_ok='false'; grep -q 'localhost' /etc/hosts && preserved_ok='true'; backup_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/hosts.bak ] && backup_ok='true'; expected=$(printf 'blocked=3\nbackup=/etc/hosts.bak\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/hosts_block_report.txt 2>/dev/null); report_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && report_ok='true'; echo "blocked_ads=$blocked_ads"; echo "blocked_tracker=$blocked_tracker"; echo "blocked_malware=$blocked_malware"; echo "preserved_ok=$preserved_ok"; echo "backup_ok=$backup_ok"; echo "report_ok=$report_ok"
